summary: Base standard of the IEEE 802 family; specifies the structure of IEEE 802 MAC addresses, including 48-bit (EUI-48-based) universal addresses. Defines the two flag bits of the first octet: the I/G bit (least significant bit; 0 = individual/unicast, 1 = group/multicast) and the U/L bit (second least significant bit; 0 = universally administered, 1 = locally administered), together with the hexadecimal representation of addresses. Approved 2024-12-11 as the revision of IEEE Std 802-2014.
title: IEEE Standard for Local and Metropolitan Area Networks: Overview and Architecture
